titlestmt.titleproper: Mario Einaudi Center for International Studies records53-5-2158
titlestmt.author: Compiled by RMC Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-04-11 23:21:51 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Mario Einaudi Center for International Studies records

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Center for International Studies

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Air War Study Group

origination.label: creator
origination.corpname: Conell University. Mario Einaudi Center for International Studies

did.abstract: Departmental files include material concerning course offerings and conferences; fellowships, grants and funding sources; department and director's correspondence;

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Departmental files include material concerning course offerings and conferences; fellowships, grants and funding sources; department and director's correspondence; and records of the European Studies Program. Also, correspondence files; files of Prof. Richard N. Rosecrance; and accounts files.

Records of of the Air War in Indochina Study Group, conducted by Raphael Littauer and Norman Uphoff, which led to the publication of The Air War in Indochina, with an introduction by Neil Sheehan, 1972

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See also the Center for International Studies Research Material, #53-5-1606 and the Ford Foundation Proposal, #53-5-1533.

emph.ref: Series III. CIS and Related Publications
Series IV. Fellowships, Grants, Foundations and Institutions
Series V. Faculty and Visitor Related Documents
Series VI. CIS Committees and Affairs
Series VIII. Proposals and Proposal Materials
Series X. International Involvement and Affairs
Series XI. Wider Cornell University Committees and Affairs
Series XV. Research, Articles, and Presentations
Series XX. Library Resources for Courses

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Mario Einaudi Center for International Studies Records, #53-5-2158. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: In 1961 professor Mario Einaudi led an effort to establish the Cornell Center for International Studies, whose goal was to support and coordinate the university's efforts in all facets of international research and learning. In 1990 the center changed its name to the Mario Einaudi Center for International Studies.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Additional material and digital versions can be found online here: http://ecommons.library.cornell.edu/handle/1813/555

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reels appear to be from a conference with support from the Rockefeller Foundation and the United States Agency for International Development. A number of individuals from Southeast Asian countries are in attendance and are introduced by the emcee on side 1 of the first reel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side 1: Harry Oshima. Side 2: Warren Ilchman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side 1: Discussion, Pol and Admin, Montgomery, Napitupulu. Side 2: Napitupulu, Pradoldej, Kaminruddin, Wong, Patten.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side 1: Patten, Sibero, Banskota. Side 2: Banskota, Vichit, Bhalla, Chira.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side 1: Chira, Chang, Ayub. Side 2: Ayub, Esman, Bhalla.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side 1: Locsin, Osatananda, Pham, Alfonso. Side 2: Alfonso, Discussion, Final Session

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Side 1: Final Session, Multiple Cropping, End. Side 2: Blank
